beats.activity: Wake up, pour-over coffee ritual, light breakfast at home
beats.reasoning: Logan starts his Friday with his signature quiet morning ritual—the pour-over is a grounding habit. Low energy from yesterday (45/100) means a calm, unhurried start before heading to work.

beats.activity: Drive to Interbay for shift lead duties
beats.reasoning: Northgate to Interbay is a straight shot south; 35 min accounts for light Friday morning traffic and the warm weather encouraging him to drive with the windows down.

beats.activity: Shift lead: open the shop, train staff, pull espresso, manage morning rush
beats.reasoning: Friday morning shift is typically busy; Logan's shift lead role means he's on his feet managing the bar and team. The warm weather will drive iced drink sales. No specific events impact the morning coffee shop rush.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a sandwich and sit in the shade outside the shop
beats.reasoning: At 77F, Logan seeks shade and a quiet break. He's frugal and social with his team, so he likely eats near the shop rather than driving elsewhere. The warm weather makes an outdoor meal appealing.

beats.activity: Afternoon shift: service, mentoring newer baristas, mid-shift lull
beats.reasoning: Friday afternoon usually quiets down mid-week; Logan uses the downtime to coach staff on technique and enjoy the social rhythm of the shop. He clocks out before evening rush and Pride festivities hit downtown.

beats.activity: Drive from Interbay toward Capitol Hill, navigate around Pride road closures on Broadway and Pike
beats.reasoning: Pride kickoff starts at 5pm with street fair and closures; Logan heads downtown with his camera to catch the golden hour and the festive energy. Extra 5 min budgeted for navigating around Broadway/Pike closures.

beats.activity: Wander Capitol Hill Pride street fair, shoot film photos of crowd and festive energy
beats.reasoning: Logan values people-watching and photography; Pride's inclusive, joyful vibe aligns with his social-butterfly nature. The warm, breezy Friday and golden light are perfect for his film camera. He's not a big-crowd person, but this feels authentic to his character—intimate moments within the festival.

beats.activity: Grab dinner at a Capitol Hill spot, enjoy a beer and wind down
beats.reasoning: After shooting for nearly 2 hours, Logan grabs a casual dinner. Volunteer Park Café is a beloved neighborhood spot; he can relax, review his photos, and soak in the Pride atmosphere without feeling overwhelmed by crowds.

beats.activity: Drive home to Northgate, avoid downtown congestion from Pride
beats.reasoning: After dinner, Logan heads home before late-night Pride crowds intensify. 45 min accounts for potential lingering traffic around Capitol Hill and Broadway closures as evening deepens.

diary:
Started the day right with my pour-over at home—those quiet mornings are sacred. The shop was slammed during the morning rush, which kept me sharp. Trained a couple of new hires on the espresso machine; one of them finally nailed the milk technique, so that felt good. Lunch was a simple sandwich in the shade, just watching people pass by on Interbay.

Afternoon mellowed out around 3pm, which gave me time to breathe and actually talk to regulars instead of just pulling shots. After my shift, I decided to head down to Capitol Hill for Pride. The streets were electric—so much color and joy everywhere. I brought my film camera and just wandered, snapping photos of the crowd. Grabbed a beer at Volunteer Park Café and felt something settle in me, watching the city celebrate like that. Drove home feeling lighter than I have in weeks.
thought_process: I chose to extend my day into Capitol Hill because the energy was contagious and I needed it. Work was solid—mentoring the new baristas gave me purpose, but I was craving something bigger than the routine. Pride felt like permission to step outside myself for a few hours. The warm weather helped too; it made everything feel possible. I think I've been running on fumes lately, and today reminded me why I love this city. The road closures actually worked in my favor—forced me to take a different route and stumble into something unplanned. That's rare for me.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
